idea-plugin.name: Pre-Push Compilation Checker
idea-plugin.description:
Blocks git pushes when your code has compilation errors — before they reach the remote.

How it works

  • IDE Push Guard: Hooks into the IntelliJ Git Push dialog via the prePushHandler extension point. Checks whether the IDE already knows about problems in the pushed files, then compiles only the affected modules. A full project build is used only when build files or file deletions are involved.
  • Terminal Push Guard: On project open, the plugin installs a managed pre-push Git hook that runs a fast Gradle or Maven compile before allowing any push from a terminal or external git client.

Features

  • Smart compile scope — module-level by default, project-level when necessary
  • IDE problem check — skips compilation if IntelliJ already reports errors
  • Navigable error dialog — double-click any error to jump to the source file
  • Refresh action — re-run the check from the block dialog without cancelling the push
  • Compilation Checker tool window — side panel showing errors with file navigation
  • Gradle (wrapper or system) and Maven (wrapper or system) support
  • Respects PRE_PUSH_CHECKER_COMMAND env variable for custom check commands

Requirements

  • IntelliJ IDEA 2023.3 or later
  • Java 17+
  • Java or Kotlin (JVM) project
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.1

  • Fix EDT violation when triggering a compilation check from the tool window Run Check button

1.0.0 — Initial Release

  • Pre-push handler for the IntelliJ Git Push dialog
  • Smart module-level compile scope with automatic full-build fallback
  • IDE problem check using WolfTheProblemSolver before triggering a build
  • Compilation error dialog with file-type icons, navigation, and Refresh action
  • Compilation Checker tool window (right side panel)
  • Git hook installer for terminal push support (Gradle & Maven)
  • CompilationErrorService for cross-component error state sharing
